methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Sublime Systems is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Leah Ellis and Yet-Ming Chiang. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the company's $80M+ total raised, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $152M. Peer-median valuation across six same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $355M, reflecting a wide dispersion (range: $120M–$5.0B) driven by outliers like Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B. The peer median is materially higher than the stage-progression output, likely reflecting stronger capital intensity and duration-of-value-creation profiles in frontier climate infrastructure.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $129M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $254M. The $129M floor represents a conservative stage-progression view; the $408M ceiling weights peer comps more heavily. Key uncertainties include technology maturity relative to peer benchmarks, capital efficiency post-Series B, and the appropriateness of the outlier-skewed peer set. The wide dispersion ($120M–$5.0B among peers) underscores that frontier-stage climate valuations remain highly dependent on perceived technical differentiation and path to scale.
